items.title: Oil Extends Decline After Trump Says US Nearing Deal With Iran
items.summary: Oil extended declines after President Donald Trump said a peace deal with Iran could be signed as soon as the weekend following two days of US military strikes that cast doubt over progress to end the war.

items.title: Dollar Drops the Most in a Month as Trump Says the War Is Ending
items.summary: The dollar fell by the most in more than a month as President Donald Trump’s vow of progress on a deal to end the war with Iran eased demand for the haven.

items.title: Trump Says Deal With Iran Could Be Signed Within Days
items.summary: President Donald Trump says a deal to end the war with Iran could be signed within days. “We just made a great settlement of the war with Iran, and we’re going to be subject to finalization of documents, we should get done over the next few days,” Trump said. Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld report.  (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Taking Kharg Island 'Very Doable' For US Forces in MidEast, Says Rebecca Grant
items.summary: Rebecca Grant, senior fellow and vice president for defense programs at the Lexington Institute, said that the US is better positioned to take over Kharg Island than it was at the start of the war and that it is 'very doable' for the forces central command has in the region. The war in Ukraine continues to devastate children’s lives.

Above all, children in Ukraine need peace.

items.title: Iran Not On List of Countries Trump Says Have Agreed to Deal
items.summary: Bloomberg's Michelle Jamrisko said that while President Trump said that a deal to end conflict with Iran has been approved by a host of MidEast countries, Iran itself is not included on that list. Jamrisko says that this may be another instance of the push and pull between Iran and the US as the two sides negotiate and that any announcement from either side should be viewed with caution.  (Source:

items.title: Asian Stocks to Gain as Trump Signals US-Iran Deal: Markets Wrap
items.summary: Stocks in Asia were set to advance Friday, tracking a rally on Wall Street after President Donald Trump said the US was nearing an agreement with Iran, raising hopes for a diplomatic end to a conflict that has rattled global markets.
